events.text: Testing infrastructure locally prevents production misconfigurations. We verify that your workstation has KVM support, libvirt, QEMU, and cloud-init tools installed for virtual machine provisioning.
All prerequisites pass. We provision a minimal two-VM cluster on a virtual network -- a bridge node and a worker node -- with SSH access ready.
Cluster provisioning complete. We check that both VMs are running, with their assigned IP addresses, CPU allocation, and memory visible in the status table.
Both VMs are online. We verify SSH access to the bridge node by running a remote command. You'll see its hostname returned, confirming the tunnel works.
SSH confirmed. We run uname on the bridge node to show the kernel version and architecture, demonstrating arbitrary remote command execution.
Testing complete. We tear down the entire cluster, destroying all VMs in reverse order and confirming a clean shutdown.
